intro: Drimia delagoensis, also known as Drimia or Wild Squill, is a flowering plant species native to tropical Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Drimia delagoensis include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Drimia delagoensis is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or made into a tincture, to treat various health conditions.
